File contains magnetic indices derived from the magnetometer data provided 
by the SuperMAG collaborators. These indices are not official indices and are 
not authorized by IAGA. The official indices can be found on the World Data 
Center for Geomagnetism website (http://wdc.kugi.kyoto-u.ac.jp/index.html).

If you use the indices in publications and/or presentations please clearly 
indicate that these are SuperMAG derived indices and reference appropriate 
publications (see below).


Caveats:

 1)	Despite comprehensive and time-consuming data correction and validation 
 	SuperMAG does not guarantee the quality of the data from the individual 
 	station and thus does not guarantee the quality of the indices either.  
 	The user is encouraged to validate the data before using them in 
 	publications.  

 2)	Indices are revised as new stations are added to the data holdings.
 
 3)	Finally, the indices are based on the SuperMAG version of the data and  
 	thus have undergone error correction, temporal resampling, rotation,  
 	and the baseline has been subtracted.  The data used are thus not  
 	equivalent to the original data that can be obtained from the 
 	collaborators.


Acknowledgement:
  
 In all cases please include: 
 •	the text:  “We gratefully acknowledge the SuperMAG collaborators 
 	(http://supermag.jhuapl.edu/info/?page=acknowledgement)”
 •	Gjerloev, J. W. (2012), The SuperMAG data processing technique,   
 	J. Geophys. Res., 117, A09213, doi:10.1029/2012JA017683.

 When Using Indices:
 •	Include appropriate reference for indices used (see list below).
 •	Include SuperMAG reference: Gjerloev, J. W. (2012), The SuperMAG  
 	data processing technique, J. Geophys. Res., 117, A09213, 
 	doi:10.1029/2012JA017683.

 When Using OMNI:
 •	Include appropriate acknowledgement 
 	(see http://supermag.jhuapl.edu/info/data.php?page=swdata).

 When Using Imaging:
 •	Include appropriate acknowledgement 
 	(see http://supermag.jhuapl.edu/info/data.php?page=images).


References:

 SMLs, SMLd, SMUs, SMUd:
 Gjerloev, J. W., R. A. Hoffman, S. Ohtani, J. Weygand, and R. Barnes,  
 	Response of the Auroral Electrojet Indices to Abrupt Southward IMF  
 	Turnings (2010), Annales Geophysicae, 28, 1167-1182.

 SML, SMU, SME:
 Newell, P. T., and J. W. Gjerloev (2011), Evaluation of SuperMAG auroral 
 	electrojet indices as indicators of substorms and auroral power,  
 	J. Geophys. Res., 116, A12211, doi:10.1029/2011JA016779.

 SMR, SMR-LT:
 Newell, P. T. and J. W. Gjerloev (2012), SuperMAG-Based Partial Ring  
 	Current Indices, J. Geophys. Res., 117, doi:10.1029/2012JA017586.


Method of derivation:

 See the SuperMAG site (http://supermag.jhuapl.edu/indices/?tab=description)

Data formatting information:

The file is broken into records (one record per minute) and contains a single 
header at the top describing the data in each column. The first six columns 
of each record give the UTC timestamp for that record.  The data in the columns
that follow will depend on the options that were selected.
        
Data gap identifier is 999999.
